title: Advance care planning documentation

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting that advance care planning was offered or completed, who was involved with consent, the person’s stated preferences, and where the plan is available, with capacity and legal context kept clinician-led.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact section supports documenting that an eligible adult was offered or completed advance care planning, stated preferences, consented involvement, and plan availability without inferring prognosis, capacity, or choices.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting that advance care planning was offered or completed, who was involved with consent, the person’s stated preferences, and where the plan is available, with capacity and legal context kept clinician-led.

source.title: End of life care for adults: service delivery — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.7 — advance care planning and involvement

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting clinician-completed review of changing health and social needs, preferences, and advance plans at transitions without generating a care decision.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact review section supports clinician-entered review, safety documentation, follow-up, and questions when actually completed.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting clinician-completed review of changing health and social needs, preferences, and advance plans at transitions without generating a care decision.

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.7.1 — reviewing needs, preferences, and advance plans

source_limitations: Eligibility, prognosis, capacity, legal validity, named decision-makers, resuscitation, treatment limitations, and place preferences remain unsupported unless entered.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
